cards.title: Meet Claude
cards.description: You know what Claude is, how an AI assistant differs from a search engine, and which models make up the family.

cards.title: The claude.ai Interface
cards.description: Chats, history, chat search, and settings — you navigate the interface like it's home.

cards.title: First Prompt
cards.description: Context + task + format: you know how to phrase a request to get the result you want on the first try.

cards.title: Conversation Master
cards.description: Clarifications, rephrasings, following a train of thought — you hold a conversation with Claude rather than firing off one-off requests.

cards.title: The Model Family
cards.description: Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, Fable, and extended thinking — you deliberately pick the model and mode to fit the task.

cards.title: Working with Files
cards.description: PDFs, images, spreadsheets, and code: you know what you can upload to Claude and what to ask it to do with them.

cards.title: Trust, but Verify
cards.description: Hallucinations, knowledge cutoff, fact-checking — you know how to verify AI answers.

cards.title: Claude in Your Pocket
cards.description: The mobile app and voice mode: Claude is available to you anywhere.

cards.title: Personal Assistant
cards.description: World 1 boss defeated: the full loop — phrase it, clarify it, get the result. You're a confident Claude user.

cards.title: Projects
cards.description: Project instructions and a knowledge base: you organize your work with Claude in projects, not a pile of scattered chats.

cards.title: Artifacts
cards.description: Interactive documents and mini-apps right in the chat — you create and publish artifacts.

cards.title: Memory and Instructions
cards.description: Personal instructions and memory across chats are tuned to you.

cards.title: Research
cards.description: Deep research with sources: you know how to pose a research question and read the report critically.

cards.title: Connectors: Theory
cards.description: You understand what a connector is, how verified differs from community, and how to think about security.

cards.title: Connectors: Practice
cards.description: Gmail, Drive, and Calendar are connected — Claude works with your data on your command.

cards.title: Document Factory
cards.description: docx, xlsx, pptx, pdf as output — you ask for finished files instead of copying text by hand.

cards.title: Data Analyst
cards.description: Upload a spreadsheet, ask a question, get a summary and a chart — data analysis with Claude mastered.

cards.title: Plans and Limits
cards.description: You know how limits work and how to use Claude effectively on any plan.

cards.title: The Work Week
cards.description: World 2 boss defeated: project → research → analysis → document → artifact. A full work pipeline with Claude.

cards.title: An Agent in the Terminal
cards.description: You understand how the Claude Code agent differs from chat and which surfaces it lives on.

cards.title: First Session
cards.description: Claude Code is installed and running — the first task is handed to the agent.

cards.title: Guardian of Permissions
cards.description: Read, write, run commands: you deliberately decide what to allow the agent and what not to.

cards.title: CLAUDE.md
cards.description: Project memory is set up: the agent knows your commands, rules, and structure without an explanation every session.

cards.title: Slash Master
cards.description: /init, /help, /clear, /compact, and resuming sessions — Claude Code commands at your fingertips.

cards.title: Workflows
cards.description: Feature, bugfix, tests, review — you frame tasks for the agent by the rule "plan first, code second."

cards.title: Git Navigator
cards.description: Commits, branches, and PRs by Claude's hand — your project's change history under control.

cards.title: The Context Window
cards.description: You know why a session "gets tired," when to run /compact, and when to start a new one.

cards.title: First Project
cards.description: World 4 boss defeated: init → task → permissions → commit. You work with Claude Code like a teammate.

cards.title: A Folder of Instructions
cards.description: A skill is a folder with a SKILL.md that Claude loads on its own based on the description.

cards.title: The Anthropic Library
cards.description: 17 official skills: documents, design, development, writing, and a meta-skill.

cards.title: Anatomy of a SKILL.md
cards.description: YAML frontmatter with name and description, Markdown instructions, and resources alongside — the three layers of any skill.

cards.title: The Trigger Description
cards.description: The description decides when a skill fires: the outcome, the trigger words, and the boundaries.

cards.title: First Skill
cards.description: The full loop by hand: task, template, SKILL.md, three tests, and iteration.

cards.title: skill-creator Master
cards.description: A skill that creates skills: you give it raw material, go through the interview, and accept the work against a checklist.

cards.title: A Skill of Your Own
cards.description: A working skill for your own task that passed every test — a tool that stays with you after the game.

cards.title: Project Skills
cards.description: You put your team's methods in .claude/skills, invoke them with a slash command, and know how a project skill differs from a personal one.

cards.title: Hook Master
cards.description: PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop: your checks always run — auto-lint, auto-test, and auto-save work without reminders.

cards.title: Commander of Subagents
cards.description: Grunt work goes into other context windows: you delegate subtasks to helpers and get back only compact reports.

cards.title: Orchestrator
cards.description: Teams of agents and background work: you split a big task into independent pieces and stop being the bottleneck.

cards.title: Master of Schedules
cards.description: /loop, scheduled tasks, and routines: the morning report and the automatic PR check happen on their own — no "as usual" in the instructions.

cards.title: An Agent in the Pipeline
cards.description: Claude Code in GitHub Actions and GitLab: auto-review of every PR, auto-fixing tests via @claude — and keys only in secrets.

cards.title: Guardian of Configuration
cards.description: settings.json levels, an allowlist without Bash(*), deny beating allow, and the sandbox: freedom for the agent — without security holes.

cards.title: Everywhere at Once
cards.description: Slack, Chrome, desktop, web, and mobile: you hand the agent tasks where they arise, and sessions travel with you.

cards.title: Autopilot
cards.description: World 5 final boss defeated: hooks, subagents, and a schedule assembled into a project that maintains itself.

cards.title: The MCP Connector
cards.description: Host, client, server, and three primitives — tools, resources, prompts: you understand how AI connects to the outside world.

cards.title: Connection Master
cards.description: claude mcp add, /mcp, the server registry, and the connector catalog — you connect ready-made servers and know whom to trust.

cards.title: The Reference Seven
cards.description: Everything, Fetch, Filesystem, Git, Memory, Sequential Thinking, Time — you know what each reference server does and what its code teaches.

cards.title: Server Author
cards.description: The Python/TypeScript SDK and your first own tool: you crossed over from MCP user to server author.

cards.title: Three Primitives in Action
cards.description: Resources, prompts, trigger descriptions, and errors that help the model — your server runs predictably and safely.

cards.title: Plugin Builder
cards.description: The plugin.json manifest, skills/commands/agents/hooks/.mcp.json components, and testing with --plugin-dir — you package extensions into plugins.

cards.title: Catalog Connoisseur
cards.description: The official and community marketplaces, the /plugin command, and a trust checklist — you choose and install plugins deliberately.

cards.title: Published!
cards.description: The end-to-end journey is complete: your own plugin with a skill and an MCP server — from an empty folder to GitHub and a catalog submission.

cards.title: First Request
cards.description: API key obtained, first Messages API call made.

cards.title: Messages API
cards.description: System prompt, conversation history, and streaming — the foundation of any app built on Claude.

cards.title: Prompt Engineer
cards.description: XML tags, few-shot, and chain-of-thought — prompts at the level of Anthropic's documentation.

cards.title: Tool Use
cards.description: The agentic loop mastered: Claude calls your tools and works with the results.

cards.title: Structured Outputs
cards.description: Guaranteed JSON matching a schema — no more parsing and hoping for the best.

cards.title: Vision and PDF
cards.description: Images and documents as API input — multimodality in action.

cards.title: Extended Thinking
cards.description: You know when to let the model think longer — and when it's overkill.

cards.title: Token Economics
cards.description: Tokens, prompt caching, and batches — costs under control.

cards.title: Cookbooks
cards.description: Anthropic's official Jupyter recipes: RAG, evals, subagents — run and understood.

cards.title: Agent SDK
cards.description: Your own agent on the Claude Code engine: query(), tools, hooks, permissions.

cards.title: Managed Agents
cards.description: Agents on Anthropic's infrastructure: sessions and a sandbox without hosting your own.

cards.title: A Product on the API
cards.description: World 7 boss defeated: from a key to a working application with tool use.

cards.title: Claude on the Team
cards.description: Claude Tag in Slack: thread summaries, answers from your docs, and drafts — right in the team's channels and DMs.

cards.title: Spreadsheet Master
cards.description: Claude in Excel and Microsoft 365: dissecting other people's models, formulas, and error-hunting — and the iron rule of checking the numbers.

cards.title: An Agent at the Browser's Wheel
cards.description: Claude in Chrome: navigation, actions on pages, and the full debugging loop for web apps — with narrow permissions and confirmations.

cards.title: An Autonomous Teammate
cards.description: Cowork: you hand over the whole task, the agent plans and executes — and you accept the work as a manager would.

cards.title: Shield Against Injections
cards.description: You recognize prompt injection, don't hand secrets into the chat, and keep agents' irreversible actions behind a confirmation.

cards.title: The Enterprise Perimeter
cards.description: Roles, policies, SSO, and zero data retention: you understand how Claude rolls out across an organization without chaos.

cards.title: Always in the Loop
cards.description: Changelog, release notes, the newsroom, and Anthropic Academy: your 15-minute monthly ritual against knowledge going stale.

cards.title: Claude Master
cards.description: The final exam is passed: a clear brief, the right tool, access control, and verifying the result — the four principles of a master in your hands.
